reason: The remediation requires editing /etc/fstab to add a mount option for /dev/shm. Changing fstab entries is a filesystem configuration change that can affect system boot and mount behavior. According to policy, partitioning/filesystem changes are classified as too_dangerous_to_remediate due to the risk of rendering the system unbootable if the entry is malformed.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; Mount failure for /dev/shm could impact applications relying on shared memory; Incorrect options may cause system instability
